item.title: Decompose CLI index.ts — 4,320 lines into per-command modules
item.intent: A 4,320-line CLI file is unmaintainable, untestable, and blocks parallel development. Each command should live in its own module so changes to one command don't risk breaking others.
item.description: Architecture review found src/cli/index.ts is 4,320 lines containing all command definitions, option parsing, and handler logic in a single file. This creates: (1) merge conflicts when multiple features touch CLI simultaneously, (2) impossible to unit test individual commands in isolation, (3) no clear ownership boundary between modules. Decomposition strategy: extract each command (or command group) into src/cli/commands/<name>.ts, with index.ts reduced to a thin registration layer that imports and wires commands to Commander.js.
steps: Audit src/cli/index.ts — catalog all commands and their line ranges
Extract each command group into its own file (e.g., deploy.ts, doctor.ts, blueprint.ts, skill.ts, cloud.ts)
Each command file exports a function that takes the Commander program and registers its commands
Reduce index.ts to imports + command registration + top-level error handler
Run npm test to confirm no regression
Run npm run build to confirm compilation succeeds
acceptanceCriteria: src/cli/index.ts is under 500 lines — purely command registration and top-level error handling
Each command group lives in src/cli/commands/<name>.ts
All existing CLI commands work identically — no user-visible behavior change
npm test and npm run build pass with no regression
